scenario: A team wants to set process variables at Start Event via Input Mapping (e.g., compute initial values, transform incoming data) -- without modifying the calling API to send them. They wonder if Start Events support Input Mapping.
question: Do BPMN Start Events support Input Mapping (zeebe:ioMapping)?

options.text: Only None Start Events support Input Mapping; other Start Event variants such as Message or Timer do not -- this mischaracterises the feature scope by limiting it to one variant. Most BPMN Start Event variants in Camunda 8 -- including None, Message, Timer, and Signal -- support zeebe:ioMapping with Input Mapping elements. The ability to declare entry-point variable transformations is a general capability tied to the Start Event element type, not restricted to any single variant.

Start Events can have Input Mapping -- zeebe:ioMapping with zeebe:input elements -- used to transform incoming variables, set defaults, or compute initial values at the process entry point. fullName, or set priority = "NORMAL" when no priority was provided. Output Mapping is not applicable at Start Events because there is no outgoing work result to project. This feature enables entry-point transformations without requiring the calling API to change its payload structure.

Start Events have no support for zeebe:ioMapping -- this contradicts the Camunda 8 modelling capabilities. Input Mapping is a valid configuration on Start Events and enables entry-point variable transformations without requiring changes to the calling API. This feature allows teams to normalise variable names, set default values, or compute derived variables directly at the process start, keeping the BPMN self-contained with respect to its variable handling.

It adds an extra element to the BPMN diagram, increases Operate visibility overhead, and separates the transformation logic from the process entry point. Start-level Input Mapping achieves the same result in a more concise and co-located way without introducing additional tasks.

a.text: Incorrect. BPMN Start Events (None, Message, Timer, Signal) can carry zeebe:ioMapping for entry-point variable transformations. Pattern: Input Mapping at Start Event: transforms / defaults the incoming variables before process scope is finalised. Common uses: Normalize naming: incoming cust_name → process variable customer.fullName. Set defaults: if priority not provided, set to "NORMAL". Compute derived: =createdAt: now(). Restructure: incoming flat → nested process variables. Output Mapping doesn't apply at Start (no work / result to propagate). Input Mapping is the supported direction. Alternative -- pre-Start transformations: Calling API does the mapping: caller sends the variables in the right shape. Cleaner contract but couples the caller. First Service Task after Start: normalises. Adds a task; more visible in Operate. Start-level Input Mapping is concise when the transformation is simple and entry-specific.
